---
name: Harvest rule
about: A governance rule earned in one repo that other repos would benefit from
labels: harvest
---

<!-- Discipline (read before writing anything):

- The expected outcome of most sessions is NO issue. Finding nothing is a
  correct, complete result — never file to appear thorough.
- A rule qualifies only if ALL are true: it is about agent behavior or
  process, not generic engineering advice; it was earned by a real, citable
  incident in the source repo; the toolkit's templates do not already cover
  it; it would change what an agent does in OTHER repos.
- Hard cap: three rules per issue. More means padding.
- Never file a "nothing found" issue.
- REDACTION: issues are public. No secrets, tokens, credentials, private
  hostnames/IPs, or personal data. Cite evidence by repo-relative path and
  commit hash.
- Agents: file only on an explicit owner go. -->

### <Short rule title>

- **Source:** <file in the source repo + the incident that earned the rule>
- **The rule:** <one or two plain-English sentences>
- **Why it generalizes:** <one sentence>
- **Proposed home:** <which toolkit template or procedure it would improve>
